title: Build IVFFlat indexes after bulk loading data
description: IVFFlat partitions vectors into lists from the data present at build time, so it must be built on a populated table.
body: Use when creating an IVFFlat index during initial data load. Do not build an IVFFlat index on an empty or nearly-empty table and then bulk insert; the lists are seeded from whatever data exists at build time, so an early build leaves poorly-distributed clusters and recall collapses. Actions: load the bulk of the data first, then create the IVFFlat index with a list count near the square root of the row count, and verify recall against ground truth. Validation signals include lists holding a balanced share of vectors and recall@k meeting target. Risk boundary: an under-trained IVFFlat index looks healthy but returns wrong neighbours, so re-create the index after any large data change and confirm recall rather than assuming it persists.
